products.title: Variegated Bloodleaf (Iresine herbstii) – Live Tropical Houseplant
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for Variegated Bloodleaf (Iresine herbstii). Discover the best light, watering, soil, humidity, pruning, and fertilizing tips.

Variegated Bloodleaf (Iresine herbstii) Care Guide

The Variegated Bloodleaf (Iresine herbstii) is a striking tropical foliage plant prized for its colorful blend of pink, cream, green, and burgundy leaves accented by vivid contrasting veins. Its bold variegation and fast-growing habit make it an eye-catching addition to indoor plant collections, patios, containers, and shaded garden beds. Easy to grow and rewarding to maintain, this vibrant plant brings year-round color to any space.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Iresine herbstii
  • Common Name: Variegated Bloodleaf
  • Plant Type: Tropical Foliage Plant
  • Mature Height: 12–36 inches
  • Mature Spread: 12–24 inches
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 10–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Variegated Bloodleaf thrives in bright, indirect light or a few hours of gentle morning sun. Bright light helps preserve its vibrant variegation and rich leaf color. Too little light may cause the foliage to become greener and less colorful.

Watering

Keep the soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Water when the top 1 inch of soil begins to dry. Avoid allowing the soil to dry out completely, as prolonged dryness can cause leaf drop and wilting.

Soil

Use a well-draining, nutrient-rich potting mix with plenty of organic matter. Good drainage helps prevent root rot while supporting healthy growth.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal Temperature: 65–80°F (18–27°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 55°F (13°C).
  • Prefers moderate to high humidity but adapts well to average indoor conditions.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce fertilizing during the cooler months when growth slows.

Maintenance

  • Pinch growing tips regularly to encourage a fuller, bushier plant.
  • Remove yellow, damaged, or faded leaves as needed.
  • Prune leggy stems to maintain a compact shape.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth and color development.

Propagation

Variegated Bloodleaf is easy to propagate using stem cuttings. Root cuttings in water or moist potting mix until a healthy root system develops.

Common Problems

  • Fading variegation usually indicates insufficient light.
  • Yellow leaves are often caused by overwatering or poor drainage.
  • Wilting may result from underwatering or dry soil.
  • Watch for spider mites, aphids, whiteflies, and mealybugs.

Pet Safety

Variegated Bloodleaf is generally considered mildly toxic if ingested. Keep out of reach of pets and small children, as consuming the plant may cause digestive discomfort.

Why You'll Love It

  • Stunning variegated foliage with pink, cream, green, and burgundy tones
  • Fast-growing and easy to maintain
  • Excellent for containers, patios, and indoor displays
  • Easy to propagate from stem cuttings
  • Adds vibrant color and tropical appeal year-round

With bright indirect light, consistent moisture, and regular pruning, your Variegated Bloodleaf (Iresine herbstii) will reward you with lush, colorful foliage and vigorous growth, making it a standout addition to any indoor or outdoor plant collection.

products.title: 2" Pilea Moon Valley – Live Friendship Plant Houseplant
products.body_html:
Shop a 2-inch Pilea Moon Valley. Learn how to care for this colorful tropical houseplant with tips on light, watering, humidity, soil, and fertilizing.

2" Pilea Moon Valley Care Guide

The Pilea Moon Valley (Pilea mollis 'Moon Valley'), also known as the Friendship Plant, is a compact tropical houseplant loved for its deeply textured, quilted leaves with vibrant lime-green foliage and rich bronze veins. Its unique appearance and easy-care nature make it an excellent choice for terrariums, desktops, shelves, and small indoor spaces. This 2-inch Pilea Moon Valley is perfect for adding texture and color to any plant collection.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Pilea mollis 'Moon Valley'
  • Common Name: Pilea Moon Valley, Friendship Plant
  • Pot Size: 2-inch Nursery Pot
  • Plant Type: Tropical Houseplant
  • Mature Height: 8–12 inches
  • Mature Spread: 10–15 inches
  • Growth Rate: Moderate to Fast
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 10–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Pilea Moon Valley thrives in bright, indirect light. It can tolerate medium light, but brighter conditions help maintain its vibrant leaf color and compact growth. Avoid prolonged direct sunlight, which may scorch the foliage.

Watering

Water when the top ½–1 inch of soil feels dry. Keep the soil evenly moist but never soggy. Allow excess water to drain freely and avoid letting the plant sit in standing water.

Soil

Use a well-draining, nutrient-rich indoor potting mix containing peat moss or coco coir with perlite to provide excellent moisture retention and drainage.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal Temperature: 65–80°F (18–27°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 60°F (16°C).
  • Prefers moderate to high humidity. A humidifier or pebble tray can help in dry indoor environments.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce feeding during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Pinch growing tips regularly to encourage a fuller, bushier plant.
  • Remove yellow or damaged leaves as needed.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth.
  • Repot every 1–2 years as it outgrows its container.

Propagation

Pilea Moon Valley is easy to propagate using stem cuttings. Place cuttings in water or moist potting mix until roots develop, then transplant into fresh soil.

Common Problems

  • Yellow leaves are often caused by overwatering.
  • Brown or crispy leaf edges may indicate low humidity or underwatering.
  • Leggy growth usually means the plant needs more bright, indirect light.
  • Watch for common houseplant pests such as spider mites, fungus gnats, and mealybugs.

Pet Safety

Pilea Moon Valley is considered non-toxic to cats and dogs, making it an excellent choice for pet-friendly homes.

Why You'll Love It

  • Unique quilted, textured foliage with bronze accents
  • Compact 2-inch size perfect for desks, terrariums, and shelves
  • Easy-care and beginner-friendly
  • Pet-friendly
  • Adds vibrant color and texture to any indoor plant collection

With bright indirect light, consistent moisture, and moderate humidity, your 2-inch Pilea Moon Valley will thrive and produce lush, textured foliage that makes a beautiful addition to any home or office.

products.title: 4" Philodendron Brasil – Live Heartleaf Houseplant
products.body_html:
Shop a 4-inch Philodendron Brasil. Learn how to care for this easy tropical houseplant with tips on light, watering, soil, humidity, and fertilizing.

4" Philodendron Brasil Care Guide

The Philodendron Brasil (Philodendron hederaceum 'Brasil') is a beautiful trailing houseplant known for its heart-shaped leaves splashed with vibrant shades of green and chartreuse. Its fast-growing vines and easy-care nature make it a favorite for beginners and experienced plant lovers alike. This 4-inch Philodendron Brasil is perfect for hanging baskets, shelves, desks, and plant stands, bringing a lush tropical feel to any indoor space.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Philodendron hederaceum 'Brasil'
  • Common Name: Philodendron Brasil
  • Pot Size: 4-inch Nursery Pot
  • Plant Type: Tropical Trailing Houseplant
  • Mature Vine Length: 4–10 feet indoors
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 10–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Philodendron Brasil thrives in bright, indirect light, which helps maintain its vibrant variegation. It also adapts well to medium light but may produce slower growth and less pronounced coloration. Avoid direct afternoon sunlight, which can scorch the leaves.

Watering

Water when the top 1–2 inches of soil feel dry. Water thoroughly and allow excess water to drain completely. Never allow the plant to sit in standing water, as overly wet soil can lead to root rot.

Soil

Use a well-draining indoor potting mix rich in organic matter. A blend containing peat moss or coco coir with perlite provides ideal drainage and aeration.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal Temperature: 65–85°F (18–29°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 55°F (13°C).
  • Prefers moderate to high humidity but grows well in average household humidity.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce feeding during the fall and winter months.

Maintenance

  • Trim vines to encourage fuller, bushier growth.
  • Wipe leaves occasionally with a damp cloth to remove dust.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth.
  • Repot every 1–2 years as the plant outgrows its container.

Propagation

Philodendron Brasil is easy to propagate using stem cuttings. Cut a healthy vine just below a node and root it in water or moist potting mix until new roots develop.

Common Problems

  • Yellow leaves are often caused by overwatering.
  • Brown leaf tips may indicate underwatering or low humidity.
  • Loss of variegation usually means the plant needs brighter indirect light.
  • Watch for common houseplant pests such as spider mites, mealybugs, and scale insects.

Pet Safety

Philodendron Brasil is toxic to cats and dogs if ingested. Keep out of reach of pets and small children.

Why You'll Love It

  • Beautiful green and chartreuse variegated heart-shaped leaves
  • Fast-growing trailing vines
  • Beginner-friendly and low maintenance
  • Perfect for hanging baskets, shelves, and tabletops
  • Easy to propagate and share with friends

With bright indirect light, moderate watering, and occasional feeding, your 4-inch Philodendron Brasil will produce lush, cascading vines and vibrant foliage, making it a beautiful addition to any home or office.

products.title: Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' – Live Pink Houseplant Care Guide
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise'. Discover the best light, watering, soil, humidity, propagation, and fertilizing tips.

Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' Care Guide

The Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' is a vibrant tropical houseplant prized for its striking pink, green, and cream variegated foliage. Its trailing growth habit makes it perfect for hanging baskets, shelves, windowsills, and decorative planters. Fast-growing and easy to care for, this colorful houseplant adds a bold pop of color to any indoor plant collection.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise'
  • Common Name: Pink Paradise Tradescantia
  • Plant Type: Trailing Tropical Houseplant
  • Mature Length: 12–36 inches
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 9–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' thrives in bright, indirect light. Bright light helps maintain its vibrant pink variegation. It can tolerate a small amount of gentle morning sun, but avoid harsh afternoon sunlight, which may scorch the leaves.

Watering

Water when the top 1–2 inches of soil feel dry. Keep the soil lightly moist during the growing season but avoid soggy conditions. Reduce watering slightly during winter when growth slows.

Soil

Use a well-draining indoor potting mix rich in organic matter. A mix containing peat moss or coco coir with perlite provides excellent drainage and supports healthy root growth.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal Temperature: 60–80°F (16–27°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 50°F (10°C).
  • Prefers moderate to high humidity but adapts well to average household conditions.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce or stop fertilizing during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Pinch back stem tips regularly to encourage fuller, bushier growth.
  • Trim leggy vines as needed to maintain a compact appearance.
  • Remove damaged or faded leaves to keep the plant healthy.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth.

Propagation

Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' is extremely easy to propagate. Simply take a stem cutting with at least one node and root it in water or moist potting mix. New roots typically develop within a few weeks.

Common Problems

  • Fading pink coloration usually indicates insufficient light.
  • Yellow leaves may result from overwatering or poor drainage.
  • Brown leaf tips can be caused by underwatering or very dry air.
  • Watch for common pests such as spider mites, aphids, and mealybugs.

Pet Safety

Tradescantia plants are considered mildly toxic and may cause skin irritation or stomach upset if ingested by cats, dogs, or people. Keep out of reach of pets and children.

Why You'll Love It

  • Stunning pink, green, and cream variegated foliage
  • Fast-growing trailing habit
  • Perfect for hanging baskets and shelves
  • Easy to propagate and beginner-friendly
  • Adds vibrant color to any indoor plant collection

With bright indirect light, regular watering, and occasional feeding, your Tradescantia 'Pink Paradise' will produce lush cascading vines with vibrant pink variegation, making it a beautiful centerpiece in any home or office.

products.title: 3" Calathea Maranta leuconeura (Prayer Plant) – Live Tropical Houseplant
products.body_html:
Shop a 3-inch Maranta leuconeura (Prayer Plant). Learn how to care for this beautiful tropical houseplant with watering, light, humidity, and soil tips.

3" Maranta leuconeura (Prayer Plant) Care Guide

The Maranta leuconeura, commonly known as the Prayer Plant, is a stunning tropical houseplant prized for its vibrant, patterned foliage and unique leaf movement. Each evening, its leaves gently fold upward as if in prayer, making it one of the most captivating houseplants you can grow. This 3-inch Prayer Plant is perfect for desks, shelves, terrariums, and small indoor spaces, adding a lush tropical touch wherever it's placed.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Maranta leuconeura
  • Common Name: Prayer Plant
  • Pot Size: 3-inch Nursery Pot
  • Plant Type: Tropical Houseplant
  • Mature Height: 8–12 inches
  • Mature Spread: 12–18 inches
  • Growth Rate: Moderate
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 11–12 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Place your Prayer Plant in bright, indirect light. It also tolerates medium light but should be protected from direct sunlight, which can fade or scorch its beautiful foliage.

Watering

Keep the soil evenly moist but not waterlogged. Water when the top 1 inch of soil feels dry. Filtered, distilled, or rainwater is recommended, as Prayer Plants are sensitive to chlorine and mineral buildup.

Soil

Use a well-draining, moisture-retentive potting mix containing coco coir or peat moss with added perlite for healthy root growth.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal Temperature: 65–80°F (18–27°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 60°F (16°C).
  • Prefers 50–70% humidity. Increase humidity with a humidifier, pebble tray, or by grouping plants together.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer using a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce feeding during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Remove yellow or damaged leaves as needed.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth.
  • Repot when the plant outgrows its 3-inch container, typically every 1–2 years.

Common Problems

  • Brown leaf tips usually indicate low humidity or mineral buildup.
  • Yellow leaves are commonly caused by overwatering.
  • Curling leaves may signal underwatering, low humidity, or cold drafts.
  • Inspect occasionally for spider mites, mealybugs, and fungus gnats.

Pet Safety

Maranta leuconeura is non-toxic to cats and dogs, making it an excellent choice for pet-friendly homes.

Why You'll Love It

  • Beautiful patterned foliage with unique nightly leaf movement
  • Compact 3-inch size perfect for small spaces
  • Easy-care tropical houseplant
  • Pet-friendly
  • Ideal for desks, shelves, terrariums, and indoor plant collections

With bright indirect light, consistent moisture, and plenty of humidity, your 3-inch Maranta leuconeura (Prayer Plant) will thrive and reward you with lush, vibrant foliage that brings life and color to any indoor space.

products.title: Button Fern – Live Pellaea rotundifolia Houseplant Care Guide
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for a Button Fern. Discover the best light, watering, soil, humidity, fertilizing, and growing tips for healthy indoor plants.

Button Fern Care Guide

The Button Fern (Pellaea rotundifolia) is a charming, low-maintenance fern known for its small, round, deep green leaflets that grow along slender arching stems. Native to New Zealand, this compact houseplant adds a soft, elegant touch to shelves, desks, terrariums, hanging planters, and shaded indoor spaces. Unlike many ferns, the Button Fern is more tolerant of average household conditions, making it an excellent choice for beginners.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Pellaea rotundifolia
  • Common Name: Button Fern
  • Plant Type: Evergreen Fern
  • Mature Height: 10–18 inches
  • Mature Width: 12–18 inches
  • Growth Rate: Slow to Moderate
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 9–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Button Fern thrives in bright, indirect light but also adapts well to medium light conditions. Avoid direct sunlight, especially during the afternoon, as it can scorch the delicate foliage.

Watering

Water when the top 1 inch of soil feels dry. Keep the soil lightly moist but never soggy. Unlike many other ferns, Button Fern can tolerate slightly drier soil between waterings but should never be allowed to dry out completely for extended periods.

Soil

Use a well-draining, organic-rich potting mix that retains some moisture while allowing excess water to drain freely. A quality indoor potting mix with added perlite works well.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal temperature: 60–75°F (16–24°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 50°F (10°C).
  • Prefers moderate humidity but adapts well to average household humidity levels. Additional humidity can encourage fuller, healthier growth.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Reduce feeding during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Remove brown or damaged fronds to keep the plant looking tidy.
  • Rotate the pot every few weeks for even growth.
  • Repot every 2–3 years or when the plant becomes root-bound.

Common Problems

  • Brown leaf tips may indicate low humidity, underwatering, or inconsistent watering.
  • Yellow fronds are often caused by overwatering or poor drainage.
  • Watch for occasional spider mites or mealybugs, especially in dry indoor environments.

Pet Safety

Button Fern is considered non-toxic to cats and dogs, making it a great choice for pet-friendly households.

Why You'll Love It

  • Attractive round, button-like foliage
  • Compact size perfect for desks, shelves, and terrariums
  • Easy to care for and beginner-friendly
  • More tolerant of average indoor humidity than many ferns
  • Pet-friendly and ideal for homes and offices

With bright indirect light, moderate watering, and occasional feeding, your Button Fern will develop lush, arching fronds that bring a fresh, natural look to any indoor space year-round.

products.title: Autumn Fern – Live Dryopteris erythrosora Care Guide
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for an Autumn Fern. Discover the best light, watering, soil, humidity, fertilizing, and growing tips for healthy foliage.

Autumn Fern Care Guide

The Autumn Fern (Dryopteris erythrosora) is a beautiful evergreen fern known for its colorful new growth that emerges in shades of copper, bronze, and orange before maturing to rich, glossy green. Its attractive foliage and low-maintenance nature make it an excellent choice for shaded gardens, woodland landscapes, patios, and indoor containers.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Dryopteris erythrosora
  • Common Name: Autumn Fern
  • Plant Type: Evergreen Fern
  • Mature Height: 18–24 inches
  • Mature Width: 18–24 inches
  • Growth Rate: Moderate
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 5–9

Light Requirements

Autumn Fern grows best in partial to full shade. It thrives in bright, indirect light indoors or dappled sunlight outdoors. Avoid prolonged direct afternoon sun, which can scorch the delicate fronds.

Watering

Keep the soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Water whenever the top inch of soil begins to dry. While established plants can tolerate brief dry periods, regular moisture produces the healthiest foliage.

Soil

Plant in a well-draining, organic-rich soil that retains moisture while allowing excess water to drain. Adding compost or leaf mold helps create ideal growing conditions.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal temperature: 55–75°F (13–24°C)
  • Hardy outdoors in USDA Zones 5–9.
  • Prefers moderate to high humidity but adapts well to most garden conditions.

Fertilizing

Apply a balanced, slow-release fertilizer in early spring or feed monthly during the growing season with a diluted liquid fertilizer. Avoid excessive fertilization, which may damage the fronds.

Maintenance

  • Remove old, damaged, or brown fronds to encourage fresh growth.
  • Apply mulch around outdoor plants to help retain soil moisture.
  • Divide mature clumps every few years to rejuvenate growth and propagate new plants.

Common Problems

  • Brown fronds may result from dry soil, excessive sun exposure, or low humidity.
  • Yellowing foliage can indicate overwatering or poor drainage.
  • Generally resistant to pests but occasionally may attract slugs, snails, or scale insects.

Pet Safety

Autumn Fern is considered non-toxic to cats and dogs, making it an excellent choice for pet-friendly homes and gardens.

Why You'll Love It

  • Stunning copper-orange new fronds that mature to deep green
  • Evergreen foliage in many climates
  • Excellent for shade gardens, woodland landscapes, and containers
  • Easy to grow and low maintenance
  • Pet-friendly and suitable for indoor or outdoor use

With consistent moisture, rich soil, and partial shade, your Autumn Fern will reward you with beautiful seasonal color and lush evergreen foliage, adding year-round texture and elegance to your home or garden.

products.title: Cotton Candy Fern Plant – Soft, Easy-Care Houseplant
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for a Cotton Candy Fern with this complete growing guide. Discover the best light, watering, humidity, soil, and fertilizing tips.

Cotton Candy Fern Care Guide

The Cotton Candy Fern (Nephrolepis exaltata 'Cotton Candy') is a beautiful, compact fern prized for its incredibly soft, fluffy foliage and delicate texture. Its bright green fronds create an elegant, airy appearance, making it a wonderful addition to homes, offices, patios, and shaded gardens. With consistent moisture and humidity, this easy-to-grow fern will reward you with lush, healthy growth year-round.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Nephrolepis exaltata 'Cotton Candy'
  • Common Name: Cotton Candy Fern
  • Plant Type: Tropical Fern
  • Mature Height: 12–24 inches
  • Mature Width: 18–24 inches
  • Growth Rate: Moderate
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 9–11 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Cotton Candy Fern thrives in bright, indirect light or partial shade. Avoid direct afternoon sun, which can scorch the delicate fronds. It also performs well under bright indoor lighting.

Watering

Keep the soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Water when the top ½–1 inch of soil begins to feel slightly dry. Never allow the soil to dry out completely, as this may cause the fronds to brown.

Soil

Use a well-draining, organic-rich potting mix that retains moisture while allowing excess water to drain freely.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal temperature: 60–80°F (16–27°C)
  • Avoid temperatures below 50°F (10°C).
  • Prefers high humidity. Increase humidity by misting occasionally, using a humidifier, or placing the pot on a pebble tray with water.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer diluted to half strength. Fertilize sparingly during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Remove old or brown fronds to encourage fresh growth.
  • Rotate the plant every few weeks for even growth.

products.title: Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema) in Self-Watering Display Pot | Easy Care Plant
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for your Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema) in a self-watering pot. Discover watering, light, soil, humidity, and growing tips.

Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema) in Self-Watering Pot Care Guide

The Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema spp.) is one of the easiest and most rewarding houseplants to grow. Loved for its lush, colorful foliage and exceptional adaptability, Aglaonema thrives in a variety of indoor environments. Planted in a convenient self-watering pot, it requires less frequent watering while maintaining healthy, vibrant growth.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Aglaonema spp.
  • Common Name: Chinese Evergreen
  • Plant Type: Tropical Houseplant
  • Mature Height: 1–3 feet
  • Growth Rate: Slow to Moderate
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 10–12 (Outdoors)

Light Requirements

Chinese Evergreens grow best in bright, indirect light, though they are well known for tolerating medium and even low-light conditions. Avoid prolonged direct sunlight, which can scorch the leaves and fade their vibrant colors.

Watering (Self-Watering Pot)

The self-watering pot helps provide a steady supply of moisture to the roots.

  • Fill the water reservoir when it becomes empty.
  • Allow the reservoir to empty before refilling to encourage healthy root growth.
  • Do not keep the reservoir constantly full.
  • During winter, the plant uses less water, so refill less frequently.

Soil

Use a well-draining indoor potting mix designed for tropical houseplants. Good drainage prevents excess moisture around the roots.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal temperature: 65–85°F (18–29°C)
  • Keep away from cold drafts and temperatures below 55°F (13°C).
  • Average household humidity is sufficient, but higher humidity encourages fuller growth.

Fertilizing

Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer at half strength. Reduce or stop fertilizing during fall and winter.

Maintenance

  • Remove yellow or damaged leaves as they appear.
  • Wipe leaves occasionally with a damp cloth to remove dust and keep the foliage looking vibrant.
  • Rotate the pot every few weeks for even growth.

Common Problems

  • Yellow leaves are often caused by overwatering or a constantly full reservoir.
  • Brown leaf tips may result from dry air, inconsistent watering, or mineral buildup.
  • Leggy growth usually indicates the plant needs brighter indirect light.

Pet Safety

Chinese Evergreens are toxic to cats and dogs if ingested. Keep out of reach of pets and small children.

Why You'll Love It

  • Beautiful green, silver, or variegated foliage
  • Extremely low-maintenance and beginner-friendly
  • Thrives in low to bright indirect light
  • Self-watering pot reduces watering frequency
  • Excellent air-purifying tropical houseplant for homes and offices

With bright indirect light, occasional fertilizing, and proper use of the self-watering reservoir, your Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema) will remain healthy, vibrant, and beautiful for years to come.

products.title: Orange Mini Rose Plant – Live Miniature Rose for Home & Garden
products.body_html:
Learn how to care for an Orange Mini Rose with this complete growing guide. Discover the best sunlight, watering, soil, pruning, and blooming tips.

Orange Mini Rose Care Guide

Add a burst of vibrant color to your home or garden with the Orange Mini Rose (Rosa spp.). Known for its brilliant orange blooms and compact size, this miniature rose flowers repeatedly throughout the growing season, making it perfect for containers, patios, garden borders, balconies, and sunny windowsills.

Plant Information

  • Botanical Name: Rosa spp.
  • Common Name: Orange Mini Rose
  • Plant Type: Flowering Perennial
  • Mature Height: 12–24 inches
  • Growth Rate: Moderate
  • Bloom Season: Spring through Fall
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 5–11

Light Requirements

Orange Mini Roses perform best with 6–8 hours of direct sunlight daily. Full sun promotes vibrant flower color, healthy foliage, and continuous blooming. Indoors, place the plant in the brightest window available.

Watering

Water thoroughly whenever the top inch of soil feels dry. Keep the soil consistently moist but never waterlogged. Ensure containers have drainage holes to prevent root rot.

Soil

Plant in a well-draining, nutrient-rich potting mix or fertile garden soil enriched with organic matter. Good drainage is essential for healthy root development.

Temperature & Humidity

  • Ideal temperature: 60–80°F (16–27°C)
  • Protect from prolonged freezing temperatures.
  • Average household humidity is sufficient, though good airflow helps prevent fungal diseases.

Fertilizing

Feed every 2–4 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced flowering plant or rose fertilizer to encourage vigorous growth and abundant blooms.

Pruning

Remove spent flowers regularly to encourage repeat blooming. Prune damaged or weak stems in late winter or early spring before new growth begins.

Winter Care

Outdoor plants benefit from a layer of mulch in colder climates. Container-grown roses should be moved to a protected area if temperatures drop below freezing.

Common Problems

  • Yellowing leaves may indicate overwatering or poor drainage.
  • Reduced flowering is often caused by insufficient sunlight.
  • Inspect occasionally for aphids, spider mites, or powdery mildew and treat promptly if needed.

Why You'll Love It

  • Bright, eye-catching orange blooms
  • Compact size ideal for containers and small gardens
  • Blooms repeatedly throughout the growing season
  • Great for patios, balconies, borders, and gifts
  • Easy to grow with proper care

With plenty of sunshine, regular watering, and routine feeding, your Orange Mini Rose will reward you with vibrant blooms and lush foliage throughout the growing season, adding lasting beauty to any indoor or outdoor space.

products.title: 6" Strelitzia Plant – Tropical Foliage Houseplant
products.body_html:
The 6-inch Strelitzia is a young tropical plant admired for its strong upright growth and broad, architectural leaves. Often grown as a statement foliage plant, Strelitzia brings a clean, modern, tropical feel to bright indoor spaces. While compact at this size, it matures into a bold centerpiece over time with proper care, making it a great long-term houseplant investment.

Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Bright, indirect light is ideal

  • Can handle some gentle direct morning or late-afternoon sun

  • Low light will slow growth and reduce leaf size

💧 Water

  • Water when the top 2 inches of soil feel dry

  • Keep soil evenly moist, not soggy

  • Always allow excess water to drain fully

🌡 Temperature & Humidity

  • Prefers warm environments

  • Ideal temperature range: 65–85°F

  • Enjoys moderate humidity but adapts well to normal indoor air

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining potting mix

  • A standard indoor plant mix with added perlite works well

🍃 General Care

  • Leaf splitting is normal as the plant matures

  • Rotate plant occasionally for even growth

  • Wipe leaves gently to remove dust and keep foliage healthy
